nodes.name: Schedule Trigger (15 min min)
nodes.notes: Do not schedule at an interval shorter than 15 minutes (SECURITY.md rate limiting).

parameters.body: ={{ JSON.stringify({ query: $json.query || 'What are the latest trends in AI infrastructure investment?' }) }}

nodes.name: POST to Node.js Pipeline
nodes.notes: Posts the research query to the Node.js pipeline. The PIPELINE_WEBHOOK_URL n8n variable should be set to the pipeline's public URL (ngrok for local dev, Render URL for hosted deployment). No credentials are hardcoded here — the pipeline authenticates to Groq and Tavily using its own environment variables.

parameters.body: ={{ JSON.stringify({ query: '__log_error__', error: $json.error?.message || 'unknown error', workflow: $json.workflow?.name, execution: $json.execution?.id }) }}

nodes.name: Log Failed Run to Pipeline
nodes.notes: On workflow error, POSTs error details to the pipeline's /query endpoint. The pipeline detects the __log_error__ sentinel and writes a failed entry to output_log.json.

meta.notes: Import this workflow into n8n cloud free tier. Before use: set the n8n variable PIPELINE_WEBHOOK_URL to the URL of your deployed Node.js pipeline (e.g. ngrok tunnel for local dev, or your Render service URL for hosted deployment). The Groq and Tavily API keys are managed by the Node.js pipeline via its own GROQ_API_KEY and TAVILY_API_KEY env vars — do not add them here. See README.md for full setup instructions.
